world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 17
major_event: The Admiral's study door opened at dawn. He emerged pale, dry-lipped, and dressed as though he had not slept. He said nothing to the prophet waiting outside. He walked to the council chamber, sat at the head of the table, and said: 'Recall the unit.' No one asked why. The order was written and dispatched within the hour.

primary_event.name: The Admiral Emerges — Recall Ordered
primary_event.summary: After two full days locked in his study, the Admiral of Tidefall opened his door at dawn, walked to the council chamber, and ordered the Dur Khadur unit recalled. He gave no explanation. The recall dispatch is now in motion, but the unit is two days' ride from the city. The window of military ambiguity has not yet closed.

supporting_events.name: Elder Ashveil Departs — Monastery Override Moves North
supporting_events.summary: Elder Sondra Ashveil left Stonebreak at dawn with two druid wardens as escort. She carries full override authority. The Monastery instruction rider, still camped at Lostfeld's outer road, has not yet been admitted. Ironmaul does not know Ashveil is coming. She will arrive in approximately two days.

supporting_events.name: Vilefin Intelligence Package — Enters the Bidding Network
supporting_events.summary: The tinker-informant reached the Vilefin market post before noon. His package — supply blackout, sealed garrison, unfinished iron frame, Monastery rider turned away — entered the bidding network before midday. Three buyers have already signaled interest: the Gloomspire Gnomes, a Monastery contact, and an unnamed southern merchant believed to front for the Shadow Court.

supporting_events.name: Rowen Opens the Bronze Tube
supporting_events.summary: Before dawn, Rowen broke the bronze tube's corroded seal alone in his cabin. Inside: a rolled vellum strip covered in the same script as the tablet and the ancient document's final pages, and a small iron key with no visible lock. Rowen read what he could. By morning he had called the navigator back. The vellum appears to describe a location — deeper than where they dove.

active_events.name: Varek and the Iron Case — Sealed Garrison, Day 2
active_events.summary: Varek did not sleep again. He ate a little before dawn and remained seated across from the iron case through the full day. Ironmaul checked the case at first light — both seals intact, nothing disturbed. The garrison soldiers now speak to each other in shorter sentences. The instruction rider at the outer road has built a small fire. Elder Ashveil is two days away.
active_events.consequences: Ironmaul must decide what to do when a senior druid with full override authority arrives at his gate. The case's continued sealed state is his only leverage. If he admits Ashveil, the Monastery may extract Varek before Ironmaul understands what Varek became. If he does not, he is defying an institutional authority that supplies his deep-mine ritual containment.

active_events.name: The Admiral Emerges — Recall Ordered
active_events.summary: The Admiral emerged at dawn, ordered the Dur Khadur recall, and said nothing else. The recall dispatch is riding south but will not reach the unit for two days. Until then the camp at four miles remains real, the garrison remains on elevated readiness, and Caeris's formal letter is still unanswered. The Admiral's silence about his reasons has unsettled his own council more than his absence did.
active_events.consequences: The recall order may arrive before the Twin Cities mobilizes — defusing the military trigger. But Caeris's formal letter demands an explanation the Admiral has not provided. If the letter goes unanswered, Caeris may interpret it as contempt and seek alternative leverage. The Admiral's council wants to know where he has been and what he decided inside that room.

active_events.name: The Proclamation Exists — Succession Crisis Deepens
active_events.summary: Caeris received no response to his formal letter before day's end. He does not yet know the Admiral has ordered the recall — the dispatch is still in motion. The proclamation remains unannounced. The Shadow Court terms remain open. The garrison remains on elevated readiness. Caeris is now balancing three live pressures simultaneously with no resolution on any.
active_events.consequences: Each tick of delay on the public announcement weakens its declarative force. If Dur Khadur withdraws cleanly, Caeris gains a window. If the withdrawal is visible to the city before the announcement, it may be read as Caeris having cleared a threat — lending him military credibility he did not earn.

active_events.name: Elder Ashveil Moves North — Monastery Override Imminent
active_events.summary: Elder Ashveil left Stonebreak at dawn with two wardens. The Monastery has committed its most senior field authority to Lostfeld. The instruction rider at the outer gate now serves only as a forward marker. Ashveil carries the power to override Ironmaul's gate order — if she can get him to acknowledge the Monastery's jurisdiction. She does not yet know Varek's full condition.
active_events.consequences: Ashveil will arrive in 2 ticks. If Ironmaul seals against her as well, the Monastery faces a choice between accepting the rebuff and escalating to formal sanctions. The druid wardens traveling with her are capable of direct action — but using them against a Thane ally would rupture the Monastery-Lostfeld relationship permanently.

active_events.name: Rowen — The Iron Key and the Deeper Location
active_events.summary: The bronze tube contained a vellum strip with a mapped coordinate and a small iron key. The coordinate appears to describe a submerged chamber below the stonework already dived — accessible only with diving equipment the fleet does not have. Rowen has the key. He does not have the means to reach what it opens without significant preparation or outside assistance.
active_events.consequences: Rowen must choose between returning to resupply and risk losing the location, or anchoring and improvising. The iron key is the most significant physical object he has encountered. The vellum's script, if translated further, may reveal what the chamber contains — or what it is meant to keep sealed.

active_events.name: Vilefin Intelligence Package — Multiple Buyers
active_events.summary: Three buyers are bidding before midday. The Gnomes are fastest and offer the most gold. The Monastery contact is second, motivated by urgency over cost. The Shadow Court-linked merchant is cautious but persistent. The tinker-informant is running a quiet auction while keeping all three buyers unaware of the others.
active_events.consequences: Whoever wins the full package gains a strategic picture of Lostfeld's internal fracture. The Gnomes will use it as leverage in future intelligence trades. The Monastery will use it to brief Ashveil before she arrives. The Shadow Court will use it to assess whether Lostfeld's situation creates an opening in the northern trade network.

active_events.name: Velorn Clan Consolidation — Shadow Court Fracture
active_events.summary: Word of Lythara's clan council call reached Velorn before midday. Velorn's response was immediate — he moved the fourth minor house fully into formal alignment and sent Arkenor's lieutenant a private message: 'She knows something. She does not know enough.' The coalition is now five houses, and it has begun preparing its own position statement for the council session.
active_events.consequences: When the clan council meets in two days, Velorn will arrive with a coordinated bloc. Lythara will be confronting a prepared opposition, not scattered dissent. If Arkenor steps fully into the open, Lythara loses the ability to isolate Velorn as the source of pressure.

active_events.name: The Admiral's Silence — What Happened Behind the Door
active_events.summary: The Admiral gave the recall order, sat through a half-hour council session without speaking further, then retired to his private chamber — not the study — and closed the door. The prophet attempted to speak with him and was waved off. The senior trade councillor who chaired yesterday's emergency session has begun asking quiet questions of the palace staff about what was found in the study before the door opened.
active_events.consequences: Whatever drove the Admiral into two days of isolation is still unknown. The recall order may close the immediate military crisis, but the political damage inside Tidefall is accumulating. If the council learns the Admiral triggered a foreign military deployment without authorization, the constitutional question of his fitness will follow.

faction_actions.action: The Admiral issued the recall order at dawn. The dispatch rider left within the hour. The Admiral then attended a half-hour council session, said nothing beyond the recall announcement, and retired. The prophet attempted contact and was refused. The senior trade councillor has begun a quiet inquiry into the study period. Cost: 3 gold (recall dispatch, courier), 4 influence (political damage from silence and deviation).
faction_actions.reason: The Admiral has decided the play is over — or has been instructed it is. He gives no explanation because any explanation creates accountability. The silence is strategic, not recovery.
faction_actions.target: Dur Khadur unit — recall dispatch sent; Tidefall council — damage containment in progress

faction_actions.action: The Dark Council received the advance unit commander's coded query before midday. After deliberation, the Dark Council sent a return instruction: hold position, maintain plausible deniability posture, await formal recall or second authorization. The recall dispatch from Tidefall has not yet arrived. Until it does, the unit remains at four miles. Cost: 2 gold (courier), 3 military (unit upkeep).
faction_actions.reason: The Dark Council does not yet know the Admiral has ordered the recall. They are still treating the deployment as active. When the recall dispatch arrives, they will reassess whether honoring it serves their interests.
faction_actions.target: Advance unit — hold position instruction; Dark Council — contingency posture maintained

faction_actions.action: Caeris received no response to his formal letter. He briefed the garrison captain: continue elevated readiness, no advance. He sent a second message to the Tidefall admiral's office — this one shorter and more pointed — noting that the presence of foreign forces near a sovereign harbor is an act requiring immediate explanation under the Hollow Accord charter. Cost: 3 gold (garrison upkeep, second dispatch), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is escalating through legal channels before military ones. The Hollow Accord gives him a legitimate instrument. If the Admiral cannot or will not respond, the charter's provisions give Caeris grounds to act independently.
faction_actions.target: Tidefall — second formal notice invoking Hollow Accord; garrison maintained at readiness

faction_actions.action: Caeris visited King Roderic again at midmorning. The king was awake this time — briefly, and confused about the month, but awake. Caeris did not speak of the proclamation. He asked about the old trade routes to Tidefall. Roderic said 'the Admiral is a practical man' and then slept again. Caeris went to his own chamber and wrote three drafts of a public succession announcement, burning each one. Cost: 2 influence (political navigation), 1 gold.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is searching for the right moment. The Dur Khadur situation is too live. The Shadow Court terms are still open. He needs one thing to resolve before he can stand on the proclamation without it looking contingent.
faction_actions.target: Succession announcement — three drafts burned; King Roderic — brief wakeful contact

faction_actions.action: Elder Ashveil departed Stonebreak at dawn with two druid wardens. A Monastery contact at the Vilefin market post sent word ahead: intelligence package available, Lostfeld sealed to Monastery rider. The contact was authorized to bid up to 18 gold. The Monastery now has an intelligence edge on Ashveil's mission before she arrives — if they win the bid. Cost: 5 gold (Ashveil travel, warden escort, intelligence bid), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Monastery is approaching Lostfeld with both authority and intelligence. They want to know exactly what condition Varek is in before Ashveil knocks on the gate. The tinker's package, if acquired, fills that gap.
faction_actions.target: Lostfeld — Ashveil en route, 2 days out; Vilefin market post — intelligence bid active

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul ordered two veterans to relieve the garrison soldiers nearest the iron case — replacing exhausted men with those he trusted most. He then formally informed the instruction rider through the gate slot that no visitors would be admitted until 'the Thane has determined the internal situation is resolved.' He spent the afternoon attempting to speak with Varek again. Varek answered one question: 'Is it still sealed?' Yes. Varek: 'Then we are still before the moment.' Cost: 2 gold (garrison rotation), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul does not know what 'the moment' means. But he is certain Varek is not lying. He is also certain that admitting the Monastery now — before the moment — is wrong. He does not know what right looks like.
faction_actions.target: Garrison — trusted rotation placed; instruction rider — formal refusal notice; Varek — one exchange

faction_actions.action: Lythara received the Vampire operative's dispatch and the Gnome intelligence summary before midday. She now knows: proclamation signed, Caeris holds instrument, Dur Khadur four miles out, Admiral absent. She summoned the Nythariel envoy back from Twin Cities via Shadow Road with a single instruction: 'Discover the Admiral's condition and the nature of the retainer clause.' The clan council remains set for two days hence. Cost: 4 gold (Shadow Road recall, intelligence cross-referencing), 3 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Lythara has identified the weak point: Tidefall's internal crisis and the unauthorized deployment. If she can characterize Caeris's succession as dependent on a foreign military presence he cannot explain, she can poison the proclamation's legitimacy without attacking it directly.
faction_actions.target: Nythariel envoy — recalled from Twin Cities for new directive; Tidefall Admiral — intelligence priority

faction_actions.action: The Gnomes bid aggressively for the Vilefin intelligence package — 20 gold offered before noon, undercutting the Monastery contact's slower bid process. The tinker-informant accepted. The full package is now in Gnome hands. They are preparing a derivative sale: the iron frame and garrison detail will go to Glenhaven; the Monastery rider refusal will go to the Shadow Court. Cost: 20 gold (intelligence purchase), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Gnomes outbid the Monastery because speed is power. Holding the full raw package allows them to fragment and resell it at higher total value than the Monastery would have paid for the whole.
faction_actions.target: Full Lostfeld intelligence package acquired; derivative sales to Glenhaven and Shadow Court being prepared

faction_actions.action: The tinker-informant completed the sale to the Gnomes and departed the market post within the hour. He left a false trail suggesting he was heading toward the Rock Plains — but is actually circling north toward Farrock, where he believes a secondary buyer exists. He carries 20 gold and a mental note to avoid Lostfeld roads for the next month. Cost: 1 gold (travel supplies).
faction_actions.reason: The tinker is professional and mobile. He sold high, sold fast, and is already moving. The Farrock angle is speculative but worth investigating — Farrock's recent watch rotation suggests elevated alertness to northern events.
faction_actions.target: Gnome buyer — package delivered; Farrock — secondary buyer reconnaissance in motion

faction_actions.action: Elowen received the Wayfarer messenger's pre-departure confirmation and the Gnome derivative sale offer — iron frame and garrison detail for 8 gold. She accepted. The Wayfarer bearing Glenhaven's neutral facilitation offer is two days from the Twin Cities. Elowen issued an internal advisory: all Wayfarers in the southern region should report any military movement near the Twin Cities road. Cost: 8 gold (Gnome intelligence), 2 gold (Wayfarer network advisory), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Elowen now has a partial picture of Lostfeld's crisis and a Wayfarer approaching the succession crisis. She is positioning Glenhaven as the calm eye of a gathering storm — which requires knowing the storm's shape before it arrives.
faction_actions.target: Lostfeld intelligence — partial picture from Gnomes; Twin Cities — Wayfarer facilitation offer en route

faction_actions.action: Rowen called the navigator at first light and together they worked through the vellum's coordinate system until midmorning. The mapped depth was confirmed: the chamber is forty feet below the current dive depth, requiring weighted descent lines and sealed lanterns. The fleet does not have both. Rowen ordered the ship's carpenter to assess what equipment could be improvised. He also asked the cook whether any crew member had diving experience beyond surface work. Cost: 1 gold (materials assessment), 1 military (crew reassignment).
faction_actions.reason: Rowen will not leave without attempting the chamber. The key is in his hand. The location is marked. He is a man who followed a document across the sea — he will improvise forty feet of rope and a sealed lantern before he sails away from an unlocked door.
faction_actions.target: Bronze tube contents — coordinate confirmed; fleet carpenter and crew — equipment improvisation assessment

faction_actions.action: Farrock's commander dispatched the held preliminary message to the Twin Cities — asking whether unusual activity had been observed on northern roads. He also sent a courier to the Gloomspire pass relay point requesting whether any unusual passage had been noted. He doubled the northern watchtower signal rotation. Cost: 2 gold (dispatch, courier), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The silence from Lostfeld has persisted long enough to become its own answer. Farrock is now in active information-gathering mode. The Gnome pass relay is the most reliable northern intelligence source available to them.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities — preliminary query dispatched; Gnome pass relay — unusual passage inquiry; northern watchtower — signal rotation doubled

faction_actions.action: The palace physician told the Vampire operative, in carefully indirect language, that King Roderic would likely not survive the coming cold season — perhaps thirty days, perhaps sixty. The operative relayed this to the Lythara network with the added note: 'Caeris visited and the king was briefly awake. He did not speak of the document.' The operative then began cultivating a second contact — a junior palace archivist who processes sealed documents. Cost: 3 influence (physician relationship maintenance), 2 gold (archivist cultivation).
faction_actions.reason: The thirty-to-sixty-day window changes the calculus. Caeris must announce before Roderic dies, or the proclamation becomes contested during mourning chaos. The Vampires want to know the exact moment of announcement — to be positioned before it becomes public.
faction_actions.target: Lythara network — Roderic's timeline updated; Palace archivist — cultivation begun

faction_actions.action: The three-day caravan suspension south held firm. The elder council received a drum-message from the southern tribal post: 'Foreign camp visible from ridge, four miles from harbor city. No movement.' The council extended the suspension by two more days. Elder Mrak proposed sending a single mounted scout to the Twin Cities road to observe. The council approved. Cost: 2 gold (scout dispatch, drum network), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Gilgeth elders are reading the situation correctly but slowly. They do not know the recall has been ordered. They see a foreign camp and they see no caravans. The scout will confirm or refute what the drum-message described.
faction_actions.target: Southern tribal post — suspension extended; Twin Cities road — mounted scout dispatched

recent_events.text: The Admiral opened his study door at dawn after two full days inside. He walked to the council chamber and issued one order: recall the Dur Khadur unit. He said nothing else. The prophet was waved off. The senior trade councillor has begun a quiet inquiry into what happened inside the study.

Ironmaul formally refused the instruction rider again through the gate slot. He spoke with Varek once. Varek said: 'Is it still sealed? Then we are still before the moment.' Elder Ashveil departed Stonebreak at dawn. She is two days away. The iron case has not moved.

The Vilefin tinker's intelligence package entered the bidding network before midday. The Gloomspire Gnomes outbid all others at 20 gold. The Monastery contact arrived too slowly. The full package — sealed garrison, Monastery rider refused, iron frame unfinished — is now in Gnome hands.

Rowen opened the bronze tube before dawn. Inside: a vellum strip with a coordinate and an iron key. The coordinate describes a chamber forty feet below his current dive depth. He does not have the equipment. He has ordered improvisation. The fleet holds anchor.

Lythara recalled her Nythariel envoy from the Twin Cities with new orders: find the Admiral's condition and the retainer clause's nature. Word that Velorn's coalition has formalized reached her before midday. The clan council is set for two days hence, and Velorn is preparing a position statement.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Ironmaul vs Elder Ashveil (Monastery Override)
active_tensions.description: Elder Ashveil departs Stonebreak with override authority and druid wardens. She will reach Lostfeld's outer gate in two days. Ironmaul has formally refused the instruction rider and told Varek he will not open the gate 'until the situation is resolved.' These two positions are on a collision course with no intermediary.

active_tensions.factions: Tidefall — Admiral vs His Own Council
active_tensions.description: The recall order was given but no explanation followed. The senior trade councillor is asking questions of palace staff. The prophet is frightened and excluded. The council knows an unauthorized foreign deployment occurred. The constitutional question of the Admiral's fitness is forming quietly but with gathering momentum.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Caeris vs the Timing of Proclamation
active_tensions.description: The recall dispatch is in motion but Caeris does not know it. He has invoked the Hollow Accord in his second letter. Every day the proclamation remains secret is a day it can be complicated by external events. Roderic has thirty to sixty days. The window is real and narrowing.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Lythara vs Velorn Coalition at Clan Council
active_tensions.description: Two days until the clan council. Velorn arrives with five houses and a position statement. Arkenor's lieutenant is expected to attend informally. Lythara called the council to signal awareness — but she may have summoned the confrontation she was trying to delay.

character_updates.name: Admiral (Tidefall)
character_updates.status: Emerged at dawn. Issued recall order without explanation. Attended half-hour council session in silence. Retired to private chamber. Waved off the prophet. His physical appearance was described as pale and diminished. He has not eaten publicly.

character_updates.name: Grand Druid Varek
character_updates.status: Second night without sleep. Spoke one sentence to Ironmaul: 'Is it still sealed? Then we are still before the moment.' Has not explained what the moment is. Watches the iron case. Eats sparingly. His altered eyes are unchanged.

character_updates.name: Thane Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Formally refused the instruction rider. Replaced garrison soldiers near the case with trusted veterans. Spoke with Varek once. Is trying to understand what 'before the moment' means before it becomes 'during.'

character_updates.name: Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Sent second formal notice invoking Hollow Accord. Spoke briefly with a wakeful Roderic — Roderic said 'the Admiral is a practical man' and slept again. Wrote three succession announcement drafts and burned all three. The proclamation is still sealed in the archive.

character_updates.name: Rowen
character_updates.status: Has the iron key and the coordinate. Ordered equipment improvisation. Has not told the crew what the bronze tube contained. Fleet holds anchor. He slept three hours and is now watching the carpenter's assessment with visible impatience.

character_updates.name: Queen Lythara
character_updates.status: Recalled the Nythariel envoy with new intelligence directive. Has confirmed Velorn's coalition is formalized. Knows Roderic is failing but not the timeline. Preparing for the clan council in two days with incomplete intelligence on all fronts.

faction_morale.reason: Varek's phrase 'before the moment' is circulating among the garrison soldiers who overheard it. No one knows what the moment is. The veterans near the case are steady but frightened. The unfinished iron frame in the yard has become a silent symbol of something unresolved.

The Admiral emerged but gave no comfort. The council has been sidelined, the prophet excluded, and a foreign military deployment is in the process of being quietly reversed with no explanation. The institution is functioning but the head is absent in all but body.

The garrison remains on elevated readiness for a second day. The proclamation is still secret. The Hollow Accord letter has been sent but unanswered. Caeris is visibly managing pressure rather than leading from clarity.

The instruction rider was refused a second time. Elder Ashveil is en route but two days away. The Gnomes outbid their contact for the intelligence package. The Monastery is moving with authority but losing information speed to commercial rivals.

Velorn's coalition has formalized. Lythara is preparing for a clan council she did not plan to need. The Nythariel envoy is being recalled mid-assignment, signaling reactive command. Three plays and none resolved.
The unit holds at four miles on Dark Council instruction. The recall dispatch from Tidefall has not arrived. The commander is professional, patient, and waiting. No anxiety — this is the work.

Won the full Lostfeld intelligence package over the Monastery's bid. Derivative sales being prepared. The Gnomes now hold the most complete picture of Lostfeld's crisis of any outside faction. This is their preferred position in any crisis.

The tube was opened, the coordinate confirmed, the key is real. The crew is curious and somewhat awed. Rowen is visibly energized by the problem. Equipment improvisation has given the crew something to do — focus replaces anxiety.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.
